clinical_history: Elderly woman with hypotension and an acutely rapid heart rate. Which ONE of the following arrhythmias is seen? (Artifact is present in lead V4.)
diagnosis: The ECG shows AF with a very rapid ventricular response (mean resting rate is nearly 200/min.) The torrentially rapid rate is unusual in a patient this age. She was hypotensive and septic, accounting for a hyper-adrenergic state. Thyroid function was normal. She converted promptly to sinus rhythm with pharmacologic therapy. The ECG also shows criteria for left ventricular hypertrophy (R wave in aVL plus S wave in V3 > 20 mm in a woman), confirmed by transthoracic echocardiogram. Nonspecific ST-T changes are present.  Of note, AF with a very rapid ventricular response is sometimes mistaken for a paroxysmal supraventricular tachycardia (e.g., AVNRT) because the fibrillatory (f) waves (which are not actual P waves, as would be seen in MAT) are difficult to discern at rapid ventricular rates and the ventricular rate may appear regular ("pseudo-regularization"). However, careful inspection shows that within this 10-sec recording, the instantaneous ventricular rate (beat/min) actually varies markedly between about 150 and nearly 250/min (count the small number of boxes [40 ms in width] between consecutive R waves and divide that number into 1500). See also Case 516.
